description_long: This object is a vintage desktop computer setup featuring a bulky CRT monitor with a distinctly angular, faceted casing and a large, glassy display area. The monitor sits atop a matching rectangular computer base unit, which includes multiple front-facing drive bays and a set of three vertical slots, likely for ventilation or ports. A classic, full-sized keyboard with a numeric keypad is connected to the base, and the entire ensemble is rendered in a muted beige color typical of 1980s and early 1990s office equipment. The monitor's screen is framed by a thick, slightly beveled bezel with rounded corners, and the base unit has visible buttons and slots on its front face. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, and the materials appear to be primarily hard plastic with some metallic and glass components.
description: A retro beige CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel, sitting on a matching base unit with drive bays and a classic keyboard. The design is angular and utilitarian, evoking early personal computers.
description_view_0: Side view showing the monitor's angular casing, the keyboard angled to the left, and three vertical slots on the base's side.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the faceted back of the monitor and the flat, featureless rear of the base unit with two rectangular indents.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the keyboard angled to the right and three vertical slots visible on the base's side.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the monitor's glassy screen, thick bezel, two drive bays, three vertical slots, and the full keyboard layout.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, angular, and slightly beveled with rounded corners, made of matte beige plastic with no visible decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by a flat, rectangular base unit that serves as both the computer case and stand, with no visible adjustability or separate pedestal.
attribute_button_placement: Physical buttons are located on the front face of the base unit, grouped together to the right of the drive bays.
attribute_port_visibility: Three vertical slots, likely ports or vents, are visible on the right side and front of the base unit.
